recent_calls_14d.subject: Zycus - Pladis - In Person Value Session

our_open_deliverables.text: Send indicative pricing proposal for UK P2P pilot (iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin Intake with/without AI optionality, invoicing, Anna iSource Lite + auction) by end of May week (original commitment from 13 May call)
Send RFP primer file to Alessandro and IT to help shape tender questions for a modern agentic-AI source-to-pay platform
Send value questionnaire (pre-questionnaire, ~15 questions on spend profiles, investment costs, tail-spend breakdown) to Alessandro within a few days of 4 Jun call

Build finalized commercial proposals (single pack with UK in-scope items and optional global/full-S2P modules) and business case (ROI model with conservative/best-case Anna savings scenarios) once Alessandro returns completed value questionnaire
Send calendar invite for in-person meeting at Pladis Chiswick Park office (Building 3) on 22 June 2026, 1:30-3:00 PM, including Zycus participants and office address
Send calendar invite for value engineering team session on Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:00 PM (virtual) including Zycus value engineering participants

Send invitation (with event details and agenda when available) to Alessandro and Jennifer Parise for Zycus September 2026 London event (second week of Sept, ~20 CPOs/heads of procurement, Moor Place near Tower Bridge)

buyer_dependent_items.text: Complete and return the value questionnaire (~15 questions on spend profiles, investment costs, tail-spend breakdown) to unblock Zycus business-case finalization for IT July CapEx submission
Confirm Jennifer Parise (Director of Procurement UKI, Alessandro's manager) attendance at Zycus September 2026 London event once agenda is shared

competitive_position.summary: Competing against SAP ECC (incumbent iSupplier, exposed on AI capability, modern UX, invoicing gaps) and JAGGAER in formal Oct 2026 RFP. Alessandro rated Zycus 'top one' (2026-05-13) pre-RFP. Zycus wedge: Anna (Merlin AI agent, £900k-£1.5M savings claim) and modern platform vs SAP ECC no-AI, plus RFP primer positioning to shape tender criteria around agentic-AI differentiation. Risk: value questionnaire overdue blocks finalized business case for IT July submission; without EB engagement SAP ECC may retain incumbent advantage on familiarity and lower perceived deployment risk during RFP.

competitors.name: SAP ECC (incumbent)
competitors.quote: We have SAP as ERP. We don't have a rebar or anything like that. For P2P, there is nothing. So ERP is where things happen. It's an ECC model from 20 years ago. We are not going to put something that in 3 years would be no longer maintained. We want to explore modern solutions. We don't want just to stick to SAP because there might be solutions where the integration is more complicated but it's fit for purpose.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: Formal RFP Oct 2026 will be multi-vendor sourcing event (Alessandro 2026-06-04). JAGGAER named as competitor in known insight from prior sweep.

competitors.name: SAP ECC (iSupplier incumbent)
competitors.quote: Pladis UK runs SAP ECC iSupplier for procurement only, no invoicing, no AI, 'really old platform'. Alessandro rated Zycus 'top one' on 2026-05-13.

Jennifer Parise has not joined any call'
champion_strength.summary: Champion strength: developing. Alessandro Barcellona (Head of Procurement Excellence, Evaluator) is engaged and advocating (rated Zycus 'top one', requested RFP primer, drove value-engineering session), but has not secured Decision Maker (Jennifer Parise) or Economic Buyer (IT/CFO) active sponsorship, and value questionnaire 13 days overdue signals potential cooling or bandwidth slip. Without DM/EB engagement before July IT submission, his advocacy is insufficient to de-risk the Oct RFP competitive evaluation.

sources: Avoma call 2026-06-04, Alessandro: IT leader submits CapEx in July, budget approval end-July (EB unnamed)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Jennifer Parise (Decision Maker) and Alessandro Barcellona (Evaluator); no EB/Executive Sponsor role
Avoma call history 2026-02-27 to 2026-06-22: Alessandro only buyer attendee across all 7 touchpoints; Jennifer Parise never joined
Known insight: 'No Economic Buyer or executive sponsor identified or engaged... business case being built for unknown IT/CFO decision-maker'
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. Alessandro (Evaluator, Head of Procurement Excellence) stated on 2026-06-04 that the IT leader will submit the CapEx request in July and budget approval will come from 'end of July' (implying CFO or exec-level sign-off). Neither the IT budget submission authority nor the CFO/exec sponsor is named in Contact Roles, Salesforce fields, or any call. Jennifer Parise (Director of Procurement UKI, Alessandro's manager) holds the Decision Maker Contact Role but has not attended any of the 7 buyer touchpoints (2026-02-27 to 2026-06-22) and has not confirmed attendance at the September Zycus event. The business case Alessandro is building (value questionnaire still pending) is for an unknown IT/CFO decision-maker who has not validated the scope, ROI, or Zycus positioning. Without EB engagement before the July IT submission, the budget approval process is a black box and the deal is at risk of silent objection, deprioritization, or competitor influence during the October RFP. Mapping and engaging the IT CapEx authority and CFO/exec sponsor is the single highest-leverage move before mid-July.

recommended_moves: Secure Alessandro's completed value questionnaire by 2026-07-08 (this week) to finalize the business case (conservative Anna ROI £900k-£1.5M, 5-6x payback) for IT July CapEx submission. If questionnaire is not returned by 2026-07-08, escalate to Jennifer Parise (DM, his manager) to unblock, citing the July IT submission deadline and end-July budget approval gate.

Map and engage the IT CapEx budget submission authority (IT leader who submits July CapEx request per Alessandro 2026-06-04) and CFO/exec sponsor (budget approval end-July) by 2026-07-15. Request Alessandro introduce Zycus to the IT leader and CFO for a 30-minute business case pre-brief (virtual or in-person) before the July IT submission, positioning the Anna ROI (£900k-£1.5M savings) and modern platform value vs SAP ECC. If Alessandro cannot secure the introduction, escalate through the deal owner's manager to Jennifer Parise (DM) to arrange the EB engagement.

Secure Jennifer Parise (Decision Maker, Director of Procurement UKI, Alessandro's manager) active engagement by 2026-07-22. Invite her to a joint session with Alessandro and the deal owner's manager (executive-to-executive connect) to review the finalized business case and confirm her sponsorship for the Oct RFP. If she cannot join, request Alessandro brief her on the business case and Zycus positioning before the RFP is released, and confirm her attendance at the September Zycus London event (second week Sept, ~20 CPOs) to build her conviction and multi-thread the relationship.
